channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Coach Tom to act as T1 Interim Head Coach as kkOma takes “temporary leave of absence”
item.content:encoded:
T1 has announced that Im ‘Tom’ Jae-hyeon will act as Interim Head Coach of its League of Legends roster as Kim ‘kkOma’ Jeong-gyun takes a “temporary leave of absence due to personal reasons.”

Tom will take charge of the roster as they begin the LCK regular season with a rematch of the Worlds 2025 Grand Final against KT Rolster on April 1.

Popular Coach Tom returns to the Interim Head Coach role

The South Korean returns to the role for the first time since 2023, when Tom took over as Interim Coach following the departure of Bae ‘Bengi’ Seong-woong as Head Coach.

Casual League fans would be forgiven if they thought Tom was already the Head Coach of T1. The now interim Head Coach is often more visible to the general public than kkOma as Tom takes the lead in the draft.

In his role in the draft, Tom has become a somewhat cult figure to League fans, with T1’s drafting comms often being shared by the organization as part of T1’s content.

One such moment went viral last year, as Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok tried to persuade Mun ‘Oner’ Hyeon-jun to play Mundo in Game 5 of the Worlds 2025 quarter-finals against Anyone’s Legend.

Oner had not played Dr. Mundo in a competitive match before the game, but Tom reassured Oner that the champion was simple to play, giving instructions on how to jungle with Mundo and when to use the ult.

That led T1 to their third Worlds title in a row, but it has not been smooth sailing so far in 2026. T1 failed to qualify for First Stand after a disappointing LCK Cup exit to Dplus KIA after a shock loss to BNK FearX in the Upper Bracket.

Tom will be hoping to focus his team’s minds as they enter the LCK regular season, which will ultimately lead to MSI and Worlds 2026.

T1 statement in full:

“Ahead of the 2026 LCK regular season, we would like to update our fans of a change in T1’s roster.

“Prior to the start of the season, Head Coach ‘kkOma’ Kim Jeong-gyun expressed his intention to take a temporary leave of absence due to personal reasons.

“After careful and thorough discussions, T1 has decided to respect his wishes. Accordingly, ‘kkOma’ will be taking a break starting from the 2026 LCK regular season.

“In accordance with LCK regulations, which require a head coach to be present on-site during matches, Coach ‘Tom’ Im Jae-hyeon will step in as Interim Head Coach and lead the team.

“We kindly ask for your continued support and encouragement for Interim Head Coach ‘Tom,’ who has already demonstrated outstanding leadership in the past, as he once again takes on this role.

“T1 will also spare no effort in providing full support to ensure that our players maintain peak performance throughout the season.

“As T1 embarks on this journey, we sincerely ask for your unwavering support and continued interest in our players.”

After a disappointing LCK Cup, will this set T1 back further? Let us know on our Discord server!

For more League news, CBLOL is set to have two slots at Worlds 2026.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Insomniac ‘Wants To Show More’ Wolverine Before Opening Pre-Orders
item.content:encoded:
Insomniac Games has revealed why pre-orders aren’t yet live for Marvel’s Wolverine, and it turns out it’s because they want to show you more gameplay.

Marvel’s Wolverine is highly anticipated, with the release date confirmed for September this year. However, with a release date usually comes pre-orders, but Insomniac seem insistent on holding off, at least until Spring 2026.

Insomniac has doubled down on sharing more Wolverine gameplay this Spring, with the news coming from an innocent reply to a fan on X. It was mentioned that Spring has officially begun, and they asked whether there will still be more information this Spring, to which Insomniac replied ‘During Spring 2026, yes.’

In the same thread, another fan asked for pre-orders, but Insomniac had a very teasing reply:

We want to show you more of the game first.

Whilst Spring technically lasts until mid June, with the Summer Solstice primed for Sunday, June 21, it would be surprising to see Insomniac hold off on new gameplay for that long. PlayStation often hold State of Plays in either April or May (sometimes both), so it’s likely fans can expect to see more of Marvel’s Wolverine then.

Whether that takes form as a Wolverine-focused deep-dive, or a generalised trailer showcase remains to be seen, but there’s a high chance that when you next see Wolverine, pre-orders should open—so prepare your wallets.

Are you excited for Wolverine, and will you be pre-ordering it? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the latest Crimson Desert patch that makes the game easier, as well as the newest character reveal for Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls.

MOBILE APP

item.title: CBLOL to have Two Slots at Worlds as Riot Announce MSI & Worlds 2026 Details
item.content:encoded:
CBLOL has been granted an additional slot for League of Legends Worlds 2026, bringing the total number of attending teams up to 19. The runner-up of CBLOL 2026 Split 2 will fill the slot.

CBLOL will continue to hold only one slot for the Mid-Season Invitational (MSI); however, that slot will now directly feed into the main bracket of the competition, bypassing the Play-In stage.

Riot denies Americas Cup influence on Worlds 2026 decision

The news comes after FURIA Jungler Pedro ‘Tatu’ Seixas sparked debate about the slot distribution following the Brazilian roster’s Americas Cup victory.

The Jungler questioned why the LCS was favoured in the distribution while being interviewed following FURIA’s victory over Cloud9, stating, “I want to understand why these guys have 3 spots, and I only have one. I just want to understand that.” (translated)

When asked by Sheep Esports whether pressure from the Brazilian community led to the decision, Riot Games Global Head of League of Legends Esports, Chris Greeley, denied that the two were linked.

Greeley stated, “No, I don’t think pressure from the community had any real influence on the decision. Even recent competitive results, like the Americas Cup, didn’t impact it either.”

Instead, Greeley cited that, “We spent a lot of time last year focused on splitting the LCS and CBLOL back out. In doing so, I’ll be candid: I don’t think we spent enough time at the outset fully considering the implications for international event slots.”

The Riot LoL Esports Head went on to explain that the decision was in the pipeline prior to the Americas Cup, and concluded, “This is something we probably should have done at the time of the original announcement.”

MSI 2026 & Worlds 2026 details revealed

The news came as part of Riot’s announcements regarding the remaining internationals of 2026. MSI 2026 and Worlds 2026’s details were revealed, sharing the following information:

MSI 2026 dates, location, format:

Dates: June 28-July 12

  • Play-In Stage: June 28-July 1
  • Bracket Stage: July 3-6 / July 8-12
  • Upper Final: July 9
  • Lower Final: July 11
  • Grand Final: July 12

Location: Daejeon Convention Center II, Daejeon, South Korea

Format:

Play-Ins:

  • Four-team double-elimination bracket
  • Winner advances to the Bracket Stage
  • All matches Best of 5

Bracket Stage:

  • Eight-team double-elimination bracket
  • All matches Best of 5

Worlds 2026 dates, location, format

Dates: October 15-November 14

  • Play-Ins: October 15-18
  • Swiss Stage: October 23-31
  • Playoffs: November 3-8
  • Final: November 14

Locations:

  • Play-Ins: Riot Games Arena, Los Angeles
  • Swiss Stage + Playoffs excluding Grand Final: Credit Union of Texas Event Center, Allen, Texas
  • Final: Barclays Center, Brooklyn, New York

Format:

Play-Ins:

  • Four-team double-elimination bracket
  • Winner advances to the Swiss Stage
  • All matches Best of 5

Swiss Stage:

  • Sixteen-team Swiss Stage
  • Qualification and elimination matches Best of 3, all other matches Best of 1
  • Three wins to advance; three losses to be eliminated
  • Eight teams advance to the Playoffs

Playoffs:

  • Single-elimination eight-team bracket
  • All matches are Best of 5

Which two teams from Brazil do you want to see at Worlds 2026? Talk about that on Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more League of Legends-related news, see our Riftbound: Unleashed exclusive card reveal.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Monday, March 23
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Monday, March 23, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 23)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 23, 2026.

Across

1 – Often-skipped parts of podcasts

4 – Netherlands city that’s home to the International Court of Justice, with “The”

6 – Stopped sleeping

7 – Contents of a bear-shaped bottle

8 – ___ Liu, gold medal-winning figure skater at the 2026 Olympics

Down

1 – Sheer torment

2 – “The ___ of Hazzard”

3 – “Catch you later!”

4 – Text reaction button between “👎” and “‼️”

5 – Nowhere to be found, for short

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 23)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 23, 2026.

Across

1 – Annoying disruption to a TV show

4 – Peace Palace site

6 – Wake from sleep

7 – Winnie the Pooh’s favourite

8 – 20-year-old Olympic star

Down

1 – ‘Dear ___ Aunt’

2 – Like a nobleman, peer

3 – ‘___ later, alligator’

4 – Laugh

5 – Missing from milatary duty

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 23)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 23, 2026.

Across

1 – ADS

4 – HAGUE

6 – AWOKE

7 – HONEY

8 – ALYSA

Down

1 – AGONY

2 – DUKES

3 – SEEYA

4 – HAHA

5 – AWOL

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-mini-crossword-hints-march-23/

item.title: Exclusive Riftbound: Unleashed Card Reveal – Revna the Lorekeeper
item.content:encoded:
As part of the release of Riftbound’s third set, Riftbound: Unleashed, Insider Gaming has an exclusive card to reveal, and it is not just a unit but an absolute unit.

Revna the Lorekeeper joins the Trading Card Game as a versatile Fury Rune (Red) Common card to help you conquer and hold your battlefields.

Introducing: Revna the Lorekeeper

Revna the Lorekeeper is a 7 Might Freljord Unit, with Ganking, allowing Revna to move from battlefield to battlefield. Revna the Lorekeeper also has an ability that allows the player to ready the card when exhausted when you spend 4 or more on a spell.

The combination of those aspects makes this card a formidable foe when it comes to controlling battlefields, as the strongly-statted unit can move from battlefield to battlefield with ganking.

That comes with a price, however, with the player needing to have 7 Energy available, additionally requiring the player to recycle one Fury Rune to play.

The good news is that Revna the Lorekeeper is a common card, so you should have plenty of opportunity to get your hands on it when the new set arrives globally on May 8.

Riftbound: Unleashed – Revna the Lorekeeper stats:

  • Domain: Fury Rune (Red)
  • Type: Unit, Freljord
  • Might: 7
  • Energy: 7
  • Power: 1 (Fury Rune must be recycled)
  • Abilities: Ganking (I can move from battlefield to battlefield)
    – When you play a spell, if you spent 4 or more, ready me.
  • Flavor text: “Nothing spots us! Not cold, not death!”

Rifbound: Unleashed new mechanics explained

Riftbound: Unleashed brings with it familiar League of Legends champs, with Champion Legends Kha’Zix, Lillia, Diana, and Ivern all featuring in the new set.

The third set of Rifbound also adds new mechanics: Ambush, XP, and Hunt.

Cards with Ambush can be played as a reaction to a battlefield where you have units, meaning Revna’s ability to move across battlefields may give a tactical advantage to your Ambush cards.

XP is a resource that can be used when built up. XP also corresponds to Levels on specific cards, which can benefit from being a higher Level.

Cards with Hunt will earn XP when they conquer or hold a battlefield, enabling your other XP mechanic cards.

Riot Games also revealed ‘Ultimate’ rarity cards, which are overnumbered cards that appear in less than 1% of packs.

For Riftbound: Unleashed, players can open the Ultimate Rare, Baron Nashor card.

Will Revna the Lorekeeper find its way to your deck? Discuss that on Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more news, see Pearl Abyss’s statement regarding the Crimson Desert AI Art Scandal.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
